reviews: I recommend him for Chem 1031 because the curves on the exams are helpful: if the class median exam grade was 65%, and you got an 80%, that's nearly a 100% after the curve. But his teaching can be rough and hard to follow.

Bloxton's class seemed overwhelming at first, but once you understand what to expect it is a walk in the park. If you go to every single lecture, I guarantee you will not fail the class and will succeed. He goes through his practice exams which are 99% similar to his own exams. Go into his office hours and he will help break down concepts. Nice guy

There was a decent amount of homework but if you don't procrastinate like I did its not too much. I only studied for one exam, I don't think his exams are too hard. However if you are the type of student that needs to study more or wants an A he gives a lot of good resources. Gives a lot of good info and prepares you for tests + questions on test.

I love stem but he made me hate it. Not to scare anyone but I had to teach myself the whole class. He is really nice but the class is very fast pace and easy to fall behind. Maybe it was just me but no matter how hard I studied, I barely made it through the course. I took him 2 sems and idk what he did the 2nd but it was rough. Good luck

Dr. Bloxton is a really kind and dedicated professor. You can tell he cares about his students. He doesn't assign a ton of grades, mostly just exams and the final, but he offers a lot of extra credit, which is great. I'd recommend participating in recitation so he gets to know you better. Overall, a fantastic guy and a class I'd recommend!

Bloxton gave so much extra credit in this class it was genuinely hard to fail. There was like a 17% curve on one exam and he just gave extra credit randomly if you showed effort. Attendance isn't graded but he gives extra credit if you go. He is really funny and cares a lot, he just wants people to try.

genuinely just read the textbook, do the practice exams and youll be fine, lectures were boring but i went for poll everywhere, and his midterms were loaded with extra credit, only took 4 quizzes in recitation (week before midterm) they were more difficult than the midterm bc theres no practice questions, cool enough professor

It's not funny to waste thousands of dollars because you were forced to take his class. He does not take making a test seriously. I take my career very seriously he doesn't. I cannot believe he made this the right answer on an exam:
D. None of these choices are correct according to Bart! Do you believe Bart?

Genuinely started scoring higher on tests once I stopped showing up to his lectures, which are honestly just timewasting simulators. His lectures are hard to follow since he insists on not using powerpoints. He also gives extra credit almost exclusively to people who get 100% on assessments

Dr. B is interesting, he's a funny guy, he just can't keep people's attention in class. People stopped going to chem lecture at 8am and recitation so he made attendance mandatory, the people that went to class got more extra credit. Homework takes forever, 4 exams, difficult but you can pass if you know what you're doing, study, and you'll be fine!

Took him for 1 and 2 and he got worse. He's super nice but if you aren't naturally good at chem you will struggle. I went to his lectures but they are a waste of time bc he literally just reads his notes that are already provided to us the textbook. He does give practice exams that are super helpful which he goes over during class.
Very diffiucult class test heavy if you are not a genius you will not pass this class.

Dr. Bloxton is a great professor! He cares about students understanding the information and emphasizes important topics that will be on tests. My semester had 5 tests which included the cumulative final. Although chemistry is hard and he has high expectations for students, I ended with a 97%. Attend class and you will do fine! Also he's hilarious!
Please be prepared to teach yourself. Completely outdated.

Lectures can be hard to follow and it can sometimes be hard to understand what he's saying. He gives extra credit opportunities and wants his students so succeed in the class, but the tests are hard and a huge chunk of your grade. You'll likely spend lots of time teaching yourself concepts from his class.

Dr. Bloxton was a great professor for General Chemistry! He genuinely cares about students succeeding. He has regular office hours and is always willing to answer questions. He also makes it clear when certain topics are important and will be tested on if you pay attention in his lectures.

One of the best professors I've had in the science department. He truly cares about students passing and offers plenty of extra credit opportunities. Sometimes curves the grades, which really helps. His lectures can be fun and engaging. I would definitely recommend taking his class!

All I gotta say is you better start studying the first day of classes make sure you pass all the tests, because that final exam is crazy. Make sure you get 95-100 on hw 100 in participation every point matters and do all the extra credit you will pass. He's the best chem professor out of everyone, make sure to see him regularly so he knows you.
All I gotta say is RUN. AS FAST AS YOU CAN.

This professor's lectures were not very helpful, so make sure to review outside of class. The exams were just like the practices posted, which helped during the exam. He is a nice guy and works out the problems on the board. Overall, he was a good professor but work needs to be done outside of class.

if you're bad at chem like me don't expect to learn anything from lecture or from this professor. He's a nice guy and passionate but seriously bad at teaching. You'll have to teach most of the material to yourself. No curves, but usually some kind of extra credit. Go to office hours for any semblance of teaching.

Chemistry is a hard subject, but Bloxton tries to make it easier by giving lots of extra credits! Lecture is important, and the tests are just like the practice exams, just different questions. Go to his office hours, make friends and study groups. He's so sweet, I love him dearly. Do the homeworks in advance, dont wait.

Chemistry isn't an easy course, and Bloxon isn't a great lecture teacher at all. But he is funny and gives an insane amount of extra credit because he cares and doesn't want you to fail. Do the study guides, extra credit, and all the owls. I would definitely recommend this teacher.

Professor Bloxton's 1021 class is manageable. His extra credit can make a whole letter difference so make sure to do them. The practice exams he supplies are very similar to the actual midterms. He loves to ramble. Just put in the work and you'll pass.

don't procrastinate, study hard, and communicate with Dr.B
also try to make friends in the class that helps a lot

He is nice and funny but he makes a lot of mistakes when going over problems in lecture and students always have to correct him which was very confusing. Exams are multiple choice but if you don't show your work you will receive no credit which was difficult as someone who relies on multiple choice to be easy.
Sent out ten hours of recorded lectures over the break. it was only for 5 points of extra credit.

I'm currently taking Dr. Bloxton for nursing, and he is literally the sweetest man ever. The homeworks can get a little overwhelming but he cares about his students a lot and his grading is pretty fair. Im just bad at chemistry so I struggled learning it but he gave us a lot of sources to learn all the materials. Love him!

I am taking Dr. Bloxton for Nursing right now as a freshman, and when I started the course I was very scared but as I got to know him, he is just the sweetest man ever, he is so sweet. But beware, the class is chemistry at a college base, it is hard, lots of homework, lecture and recitation too, but he helps. Office hours helps, and I adore him!!!!
His accent is sometimes hard to understand, and his writing is literal chicken scratch. He will not give you a chance to redo any homework. He moves too quickly in lectures.

The class is challenging but doable with dedication. Reading the textbook, attending recitations, and using unstuckstudy for practice are key. The professor is caring but sets high expectations. Overall, a rewarding experience.

I took Gen Chem 1 with Bloxton back in Fall 2022 so please forgive me for the late review. This man is truly the best professor in the Gen Chem dept! From calling in Bart to his other quirky moments that I can't recall right now, I can say that Bloxton is truly a hilarious and passionate chem professor! My ranking of midterm difficulty: 1

Sorry guys, my ranking for the difficulty of his midterm exams was cut off. I will just post it here (least to most difficult): 3rd midterm < 4th midterm <  2nd midterm < 1st midterm. This is just my subjective opinion of how hard his midterms were so please don't let this base any of your decisions! Good luck everyone!

He seemed cool at first, but I was wrong. Although very nice, he was very disorganized, lectures were unhelpful, and exams were graded weirdly since answer choices didn't correlate with question, leaving the class average to be in 50s-60s range. What is taught in lecture never correlates with exam questions. He offers extra credit, which is cool.

He's a witty guy I'll give him that!! Very passionate about what he teaches. Makes sure you understand the material. The lectures can (most of the time) just be him reading off of powerpoints, so sometimes it gets really boring. Good at making sure we review before exams, which has truly helped me! Very lenient and understanding as well.

I will say the only reason I did well in the class was because I remembered a bunch of stuff from high school. Hell started to break lose when we learning new stuff. I highly recommend reading the textbook and going to multiple recitations if you need help.

He's a sweet man but very disorganized. Every other class I take It's reading, homework, lecture. This guy is all over the place. He also changed the weight of our first exam from 8 percent to 16 percent in the middle of the year. I honestly thought he'd be better because everyone said he's the best chem professor.
good luck.

Honestly, great professor and cares deeply about his students. He often tries to remind students of due dates and any additional resources outside of class to pass his exams. He gives out a ton of extra credits which can really shape your grade. Downsides to his class is three exams adding up to 80% of your course grade and unorganized office hours

Idk how to feel about Bloxton, such a nice guy, just not the best professor. He like wants the student to be challenged and struggle, like he wants to really chanllege the mind, but it's like sir I need to pass this class Yk lol. He does give extra credit which is great! My only advice is to be super attentive, study, get a tutor and make friends.

Professor Bloxton is a very nice guy and cares a lot about his students. He is very accessible outside of class and offers a lot of office hours at different times throughout the week. He also offers a lot of extra credit opportunities. There is two midterms and one final.

If you would like to learn chemistry do not take it with this professor. During the lectures he writes problems on the board and reads slides word for word without any explanation. You will teach yourself in this class and spend hours on top of the hour long homework assignments. Office hours are no help you are better off taking it somewhere else.
He usually cramps a lot of information into one class, he ends class late, sweet man but I personally wouldn't take him again

He's a funny guy, but his lectures are really heavy. Please make sure you read the book. It costs around $130 to get the book and do the homework. They have over 30 questions so please DO NOT do them the day before it is due. You will stay up. He DOES NOT teach at all, he only does questions based on the reading. He is very chill but very harsh.

Great guy but sometimes I just lose him. If you can't understand what he is saying just don't go to lecture and read the book and look at recitation worksheets to study. He's a slow lecturer but cares about his students. He has a weird sense of humor.

Bloxton is very caring and replies to emails within the same day. He offers extra credit opportunities so that's great. Definitely would recommend not skipping lecture because he goes over problems and how to solve them. Make use of the textbook as well as supplementary work prior to the exams!

Honestly, Bloxton isn't that bad. I learned that we have to read the textbook before the lecture and he makes that super clear himself. He knows the course is hard and he does everything he can to help the students get a higher score. He gives tons of extra credit opportunities. He doesn't teach very well, but reading the textbook will help.

This course required a decent amount of work, however it was by no means unmanageable. His exams require that you pay the utmost attention to wording/small details of the answer choices. He is accessible outside of class and is extremely caring of his students. Recommendation: using Tyler Dewitt's youtube videos as supplemental material.

Honestly, I do not think that Professor Bloxton is a bad person or professor. Bloxton is very lecture heavy but his lectures are pointless. He is more likely to write out equations on the board than actually explain concepts. His classes typically score the lowest on the exams as a result of this. Be prepared to teach yourself before exams.

Lectures are confusing and he gets off track easily. Has horrible handwriting so I can barely take down or read the notes he writes down for us to copy. All we do in lecture is do poll questions and listen to him talk. I don't really learn much from lecture, but he is good one on one when you have questions and when we have review sessions.

A professor is supposed to be professor-ing but this man thinks doing poll questions and solving them is everything. He himself has to be writing these reviews because there's just no way. Class literally is about just showing up, watching him solve poll questions, going back home in agonizing pain, and learning everything by yourself.

Dr Bloxton is so precious with how excited he gets about a subject he's been teaching for so long. He loves his job and he does a good job at it, too. During office hours he is busy, but always willing to make time to help, even outside of office hours. Very easy to talk to as well outside of chemistry related topics.

Great prof! He is really helpful in his office hours and takes the time to know each student who comes. He knows chem is a hard course but does his best to help out students. Mostly goes over practice problems in lectures which is helpful for exams
Bloxton is honestly a great teacher. The tests are honestly quite hard if you aren't sure about what you're doing so you kind of have to teach yourself sometimes. Otherwise he's a good teacher

Professor Bloxton is a sweetheart and will do everything to make sure his students succeed. However, don't let that fool you his class is extremely difficult specifically his exams. He is very accessible outside of class so utilize his office hours because he really does care and wants every to pass his class. Btw don't skip his lectures.

Dr. Bloxton could be better at instruction because he moves super slow and does the easiest problems in classes. Tests are hard and are graded on a 3 point scale, meaning you either get a 0%, 33%, 66%, or 100%, which can mess up your grade since there's 4 tests each worth 5%. Other than that its an easy course to pass.

Dr. Bloxton is a good professor, and he explains concepts in detail. Lectures are definitely more interesting if you ask questions to stay engaged, otherwise he'll mostly read off his slides (not his fault, it's an intro chem class). He gives you a lot of chances to boost your grade, so take them!

Bloxton only reads off the powerpoint during lecture, and there are too many students for him to care about them personally. His exams are waaaay too difficult for an intro class, and half the material is too complicated as well. There was far too much information and specificity for this level of a course. I heard the next chem class was easier.
I never showed up to class and basically studied one day a week and got an A-. If you go to class and study, there is no excuse for you to not get an A. Dr Bloxton is the GOAT.

He's a sweetheart of a person, and genuinely helps you when you need it. It's pretty hard to fail his class, you literally have to not show up, you get so many attempts and chances, that just passing should be no issue, and getting an A requires a little work.
